{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "57e72b59",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Eigenmode analysis: the pillbox cavity\n",
    "\n",
    "The pillbox — a plain cylindrical cavity — is the one geometry whose resonant\n",
    "spectrum is known in closed form, so it is the natural place to both *learn* the\n",
    "eigenmode API and *check* that the solver is telling the truth.\n",
    "\n",
    "This notebook is live: every figure below is produced by running `cavsim2d`.\n",
    "\n",
    "We will\n",
    "\n",
    "1. build the cavity and look at its geometry and mesh,\n",
    "2. solve for the fundamental mode and plot its field,\n",
    "3. collect the results into a `DataFrame` with `eigenmode.qois_df`, and\n",
    "4. solve the first **100 modes** for azimuthal orders $m = 0 \\dots 8$ and compare\n",
    "   every one of them against the analytic spectrum.\n",
    "\n",
    "## The physics in one paragraph\n",
    "\n",
    "For an axisymmetric cavity the fields separate in the azimuthal angle $\\varphi$,\n",
    "so each mode carries an integer **azimuthal order** $m$ — $m=0$ is the *monopole*\n",
    "(accelerating) family, $m=1$ the *dipole* (deflecting) family, and so on. For each\n",
    "$m$ the modes split into **TM** ($H_z = 0$, these accelerate) and **TE**\n",
    "($E_z = 0$, these do not). `cavsim2d` solves all of them with a single\n",
    "product-space formulation, so the monopole TE modes are there too — see\n",
    "[Theory](../../theory).\n",
    "\n",
    "For a pillbox of radius $R$ and length $L$ the frequencies are exactly\n",
    "\n",
    "$$\n",
    "f_{mnp} = \\frac{c}{2\\pi}\\sqrt{\\left(\\frac{\\chi_{mn}}{R}\\right)^2 + \\left(\\frac{p\\pi}{L}\\right)^2}\n",
    "$$\n",
    "\n",
    "with $\\chi_{mn}$ the $n$-th zero of $J_m$ for TM modes ($p \\ge 0$), and of $J_m'$\n",
    "for TE modes ($p \\ge 1$)."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 1,
   "id": "d11514fe",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-13T21:46:52.040764Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-13T21:46:52.040629Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-13T21:46:53.554567Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-13T21:46:53.553525Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import os\n",
    "import tempfile\n",
    "\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "from scipy.special import jn_zeros, jnp_zeros\n",
    "\n",
    "from cavsim2d import CircularWaveguide\n",
    "from cavsim2d.utils.style import apply_style, WARM\n",
    "\n",
    "apply_style()          # the house publication style"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "4ca15b1a",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 1. Build the cavity\n",
    "\n",
    "A pillbox is a `CircularWaveguide` — a PEC cylinder of radius `R` and length `L`."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "e8b194b6",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-13T21:46:53.557177Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-13T21:46:53.556911Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-13T21:46:53.662603Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-13T21:46:53.661732Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 800x300 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "R, L = 230.0, 200.0            # mm\n",
    "cav = CircularWaveguide(R, L)\n",
    "cav.name = 'pillbox'\n",
    "\n",
    "# A cavity analysed on its own writes its results to a workspace folder. Point it\n",
    "# at a temporary one so running these docs leaves nothing behind; drop this line\n",
    "# and it lands in ./pillbox/ next to you, ready for cav.save(...).\n",
    "cav.set_workspace(os.path.join(tempfile.mkdtemp(), 'pillbox'))\n",
    "\n",
    "cav.plot('geometry')\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "b86ae838",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 2. Solve for the fundamental\n",
    "\n",
    "`cav.eigenmode.run(...)` takes a config dict. The defaults are sensible, so we only\n",
    "set what we care about: the monopole family, a handful of modes, and the mesh\n",
    "(element size `h` in mm and polynomial order `p`)."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "30c3b84e",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-13T21:46:53.664128Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-13T21:46:53.663970Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-13T21:46:58.776973Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-13T21:46:58.776455Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "fundamental : 498.881 MHz\n",
      "R/Q         : 220.33 Ohm\n",
      "Q           : 36652\n",
      "Epk/Eacc    : 1.208\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "cav.eigenmode.run({\n",
    "    'polarisation': 'monopole',\n",
    "    'n_modes': 5,\n",
    "    'boundary_conditions': 'mm',\n",
    "    'mesh_config': {'h': 15, 'p': 3},\n",
    "})\n",
    "\n",
    "qois = cav.eigenmode.qois\n",
    "print(f\"fundamental : {qois['freq [MHz]']:.3f} MHz\")\n",
    "print(f\"R/Q         : {qois['R/Q [Ohm]']:.2f} Ohm\")\n",
    "print(f\"Q           : {qois['Q []']:.0f}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Epk/Eacc    : {qois['Epk/Eacc []']:.3f}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "9e65d0db",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "The analytic TM$_{010}$ frequency of this pillbox is $c\\,\\chi_{01}/(2\\pi R)$ — let us\n",
    "check it, and then look at the mesh the solver actually used and the field it found."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "ce45f5eb",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-13T21:46:58.778801Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-13T21:46:58.778644Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-13T21:46:58.781683Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-13T21:46:58.781076Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "analytic TM010 : 498.881 MHz\n",
      "cavsim2d       : 498.881 MHz\n",
      "relative error : 2.22e-12\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "c0 = 299792458.0\n",
    "f_TM010 = c0 * jn_zeros(0, 1)[0] / (2 * np.pi * R * 1e-3) * 1e-6\n",
    "print(f\"analytic TM010 : {f_TM010:.3f} MHz\")\n",
    "print(f\"cavsim2d       : {qois['freq [MHz]']:.3f} MHz\")\n",
    "print(f\"relative error : {abs(qois['freq [MHz]'] - f_TM010) / f_TM010:.2e}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "17566357",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-13T21:46:58.783014Z",

     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-13T21:46:58.809250Z"

    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# the accelerating field of the fundamental: |E| peaks on axis, as it must\n",
    "cav.show_fields(mode=0, which='E')"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "de1fa98e",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 3. Results as a table: `qois_df`\n",
    "\n",
    "`cav.eigenmode.qois` gives the fundamental, and `cav.eigenmode.mpole_qois(pol)` gives\n",
    "a dict per polarisation — handy for lookups, awkward for plotting. **`cav.eigenmode.qois_df`**\n",
    "returns *every* mode of *every* solved polarisation as a single `DataFrame`, so the\n",
    "results filter and plot like any other table.\n",
    "\n",
    "Each row carries all the QOIs plus\n",
    "\n",
    "| column | meaning |\n",
    "| --- | --- |\n",
    "| `m` | azimuthal order (0 monopole, 1 dipole, …) |\n",
    "| `polarisation` | its name |\n",
    "| `mode` | mode index within that polarisation |\n",
    "| `mode_index` | `'<m>-<mode>'`, e.g. `'0-0'` = monopole fundamental |"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 7,
   "id": "aa1c16d1",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {

    }

    "df = cav.eigenmode.qois_df\n",

  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "fbe68683",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 4. The whole spectrum: 100 modes, $m = 0 \\dots 8$\n",
    "\n",
    "Now the real test. We ask for the first 100 modes of each azimuthal order from the\n",
    "monopole up to $m=8$ — 900 modes in total — in **one** call, by passing a list of\n",
    "azimuthal orders as the polarisation.\n",
    "\n",
    "This takes a few minutes."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 8,
   "id": "c9c4df6b",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-13T21:46:58.882417Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-13T21:46:58.882294Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-13T21:56:48.871190Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-13T21:56:48.870473Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "918 modes across 9 polarisations\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "cav.eigenmode.run({\n",
    "    'polarisation': list(range(9)),      # m = 0, 1, ..., 8\n",
    "    'n_modes': 100,\n",
    "    'rerun': True,\n",
    "    'boundary_conditions': 'mm',\n",
    "    'mesh_config': {'h': 25, 'p': 3},\n",
    "})\n",
    "\n",
    "df = cav.eigenmode.qois_df\n",
    "print(f\"{len(df)} modes across {df['polarisation'].nunique()} polarisations\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "d2ac7a61",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "The closed-form spectrum, for comparison — TM ($p \\ge 0$) and TE ($p \\ge 1$) merged\n",
    "and sorted, exactly as the solver returns them:"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 9,
   "id": "2aeadb1b",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-13T21:56:48.874207Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-13T21:56:48.874057Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-13T21:56:48.877200Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-13T21:56:48.876792Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "def analytic_modes(m, R_mm, L_mm, n_modes):\n",
    "    '''Lowest *n_modes* pillbox frequencies [MHz] for azimuthal order m.'''\n",
    "    Rm, Lm = R_mm * 1e-3, L_mm * 1e-3\n",
    "    f = []\n",
    "    for chi in jn_zeros(m, 20):                    # TM_mnp, p >= 0\n",
    "        f += [c0 / (2 * np.pi) * np.hypot(chi / Rm, p * np.pi / Lm) * 1e-6\n",
    "              for p in range(0, 20)]\n",
    "    for chi in jnp_zeros(m, 20):                   # TE_mnp, p >= 1\n",
    "        f += [c0 / (2 * np.pi) * np.hypot(chi / Rm, p * np.pi / Lm) * 1e-6\n",
    "              for p in range(1, 20)]\n",
    "    return np.sort(f)[:n_modes]"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 10,
   "id": "c87ab8f8",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-13T21:56:48.878484Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-13T21:56:48.878374Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-13T21:56:49.360488Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-13T21:56:49.359856Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1300x1100 with 9 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "fig, axs = plt.subplots(3, 3, figsize=(13, 11), sharex=True)\n",
    "\n",
    "for m, ax in zip(range(9), axs.ravel()):\n",
    "    num = df[df['m'] == m].sort_values('freq [MHz]')['freq [MHz]'].to_numpy()\n",
    "    ana = analytic_modes(m, R, L, len(num))\n",
    "    idx = np.arange(len(num))\n",
    "\n",
    "    ax.plot(idx, ana, lw=0, marker='o', ms=6, mfc='none', mec='k', mew=0.9,\n",
    "            label='analytic')\n",
    "    ax.plot(idx, num, lw=0, marker='o', ms=2.5, color=WARM[0], label='cavsim2d')\n",
    "    ax.set_title(f'$m = {m}$')\n",
    "\n",
    "for ax in axs[-1]:\n",
    "    ax.set_xlabel('mode index')\n",
    "for ax in axs[:, 0]:\n",
    "    ax.set_ylabel('frequency [MHz]')\n",
    "axs[0, 0].legend(loc='lower right')\n",
    "fig.suptitle('Pillbox: first 100 modes for each azimuthal order, '\n",
    "             'cavsim2d vs the closed form', y=0.995)\n",
    "fig.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "36978a6d",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "The agreement holds right across the spectrum. It is not *uniform*, though, and it is worth\n",
    "being precise about why: the fundamental matches to ~$10^{-10}$, but the error grows with mode\n",
    "number, reaching about a percent by mode 100. Nothing is wrong — a fixed mesh simply has far\n",
    "fewer elements per wavelength for the 100th mode than for the first. Refine the mesh (lower `h`,\n",
    "raise `p`, or use `cav.study_mesh_convergence`) and the whole curve tightens.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 11,
   "id": "85559059",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-13T21:56:49.362069Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-13T21:56:49.361935Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-13T21:56:49.377148Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-13T21:56:49.376667Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "m = 0:  max relative error over 100 modes = 7.77e-03\n",
      "m = 1:  max relative error over 100 modes = 4.71e-03\n",
      "m = 2:  max relative error over 100 modes = 1.11e-02\n",
      "m = 3:  max relative error over 100 modes = 9.80e-03\n",
      "m = 4:  max relative error over 100 modes = 5.22e-03\n",
      "m = 5:  max relative error over 100 modes = 9.12e-03\n",
      "m = 6:  max relative error over 100 modes = 8.65e-03\n",
      "m = 7:  max relative error over 100 modes = 8.89e-03\n",
      "m = 8:  max relative error over 100 modes = 8.47e-03\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "for m in range(9):\n",
    "    num = df[df['m'] == m].sort_values('freq [MHz]')['freq [MHz]'].to_numpy()[:100]\n",
    "    ana = analytic_modes(m, R, L, len(num))\n",
    "    err = np.abs(num - ana) / ana\n",
    "    print(f\"m = {m}:  max relative error over {len(num)} modes = {err.max():.2e}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "c7adaff0",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 5. Which modes actually accelerate?\n",
    "\n",
    "A physical payoff that falls straight out of the table. $R/Q$ measures how strongly a\n",
    "mode accelerates a beam on axis. **TE modes have no $E_z$, so they cannot accelerate**\n",
    "— and this is true for the monopole TE modes as much as the dipole ones. Colour the\n",
    "monopole spectrum by $R/Q$ and the TE modes drop out at zero:"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 12,
   "id": "f6f101d6",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-13T21:56:49.378596Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-13T21:56:49.378471Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-13T21:56:49.457596Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-13T21:56:49.457208Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 900x450 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "39 of 102 monopole modes are TE (R/Q = 0)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "mono = df[df['m'] == 0].sort_values('freq [MHz]')\n",
    "is_te = mono['R/Q [Ohm]'] < 1e-6          # TE: no longitudinal field\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4.5))\n",
    "ax.stem(mono.loc[~is_te, 'freq [MHz]'], mono.loc[~is_te, 'R/Q [Ohm]'],\n",
    "        basefmt=' ', linefmt=WARM[0], markerfmt='o', label='TM (accelerating)')\n",
    "ax.plot(mono.loc[is_te, 'freq [MHz]'], np.zeros(is_te.sum()),\n",
    "        lw=0, marker='x', ms=7, color='k', label='TE (no $E_z$)')\n",
    "\n",
    "ax.set_xlim(0, 3000)\n",
    "ax.set_ylim(bottom=0)\n",
    "ax.set_xlabel('frequency [MHz]')\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel(r'$R/Q$ [$\\Omega$]')\n",
    "ax.set_title('Monopole spectrum: only the TM modes accelerate')\n",
    "ax.legend()\n",
    "fig.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"{is_te.sum()} of {len(mono)} monopole modes are TE (R/Q = 0)\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "aa092631",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "Those TE crosses are worth a second look: an $(E_r, E_z)$-only formulation has no\n",
    "azimuthal unknown at all, so a purely azimuthal $E_\\varphi$ field is *invisible* to it —\n",
    "the monopole TE modes are not merely inaccurate, they are absent. `cavsim2d` solves\n",
    "every $m$ on the same product space, so they are simply there, and correctly report\n",
    "$R/Q = 0$.\n",
    "\n",
    "## Where to go next\n",
    "\n",
    "- [Eigenmode](../../eigenmode) — the full configuration reference.\n",
    "- [Theory](../../theory) — the weak form behind the solver.\n",
    "- `cav.study_mesh_convergence(...)` — adaptive mesh refinement and error-vs-DOF convergence.\n",
    "- `cav.plot_dispersion(...)` — Brillouin diagrams for multi-cell cavities."
   ]
  }
 ],
